unit_23 0% 正确率 0 复习 0 错题

unit_23

推荐练习默认不提示本单元词。需要复盘时可手动显示本单元词；提交后判分，也可放弃查看答案。

50 推荐题 374 全部题目 0 弱相关题 30% 推荐答案命中比例
#1 Text Completion
Unquestionably, the particular forms that folly and cruelty take in Jane Austen’s novels are (i)______ the character’s social milieu, which was also Austen’s own; but to realize that one’s society motivates people in unfortunate ways is not necessarily to (ii)______ it, for the alternatives, though different, might be no more (iii)______.Unquestionably, the particular forms that folly and cruelty take in Jane Austen’s novels are (i)______ the character’s social milieu, which was also Austen’s own; but to realize that one’s society motivates people in unfortunate ways is not necessarily to (ii)______ it, for the alternatives, though different, might be no more (iii)______.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, E, G　shaped by; condemn; salutary
milieunovelrationalize
milieu: standard unit headword | novel: standard unit headword | rationalize: standard unit headword
#2 Sentence Equivalence
One of the greatest hoaxes in the history of science was perpetrated by the Indian paleontologist Dr. Chandra Gupta, who fabricated entirely ______ fossils throughout his career.One of the greatest hoaxes in the history of science was perpetrated by the Indian paleontologist Dr. Chandra Gupta, who fabricated entirely ______ fossils throughout his career.
答案：A, F　counterfeit; spurious
plausiblespuriousrefractory
plausible: standard unit headword | spurious: standard unit headword | refractory: standard unit headword
#3 TC TRIPLE
Although often criticized by former allies for having abandoned or betrayed their common cause, Nguyen was actually remarkably (i) over the years. Examination of her career shows that she never ceased to use the best data she could find to illuminate the issues. If the data undermined positions she had previously (ii) she followed the data. If the data confirmed her earlier views, she clearly stated the fact. If the data were (iii) , she counseled caution.Although often criticized by former allies for having abandoned or betrayed their common cause, Nguyen was actually remarkably (i) over the years. Examination of her career shows that she never ceased to use the best data she could find to illuminate the issues. If the data undermined positions she had previously (ii) she followed the data. If the data confirmed her earlier views, she clearly stated the fact. If the data were (iii) , she counseled caution.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, D, H　consistent; espoused; insufficient
issuebetray
issue: standard unit headword | betray: standard unit headword
#4 SE
In the ancient world, preferences concerning perfumery were___________ : the Roman author Pliny noted that the popular iris perfume of Corinth was eventually supplanted by a succession of other fragrances.In the ancient world, preferences concerning perfumery were___________ : the Roman author Pliny noted that the popular iris perfume of Corinth was eventually supplanted by a succession of other fragrances.
答案：C, E　ephemeral; transitory
eccentricsupplant
eccentric: standard unit headword | supplant: standard unit headword

In denying her a promotion, management cited her _________ decision making, charging that her opinions were based on passing whims rather than careful forethought.In denying her a promotion, management cited her _________ decision making, charging that her opinions were based on passing whims rather than careful forethought.
答案：C　capricious
whimprecocious
whim: standard unit headword | precocious: standard unit headword

Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, F　in overall equilibrium; fluctuations
fluctuationcharacterize
fluctuation: standard unit headword | characterize: standard unit headword
#7 Text Completion
A certain amount of theoretical frenzies about comics today is (i)______. After all, similar frenzies have been seen in other art forms in the process of their rapid development, for instance, the debating about the paintings that roiled Renaissance Italy. But such intellectual (ii)______ rarely preceded creative glory. On the contrary, it indicates that an artistic (iii)______, having been made and recognized, is over, and that a process of increasingly strained emulation and diminishing returns is set in.A certain amount of theoretical frenzies about comics today is (i)______. After all, similar frenzies have been seen in other art forms in the process of their rapid development, for instance, the debating about the paintings that roiled Renaissance Italy. But such intellectual (ii)______ rarely preceded creative glory. On the contrary, it indicates that an artistic (iii)______, having been made and recognized, is over, and that a process of increasingly strained emulation and diminishing returns is set in.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, H　understandable; arousal; breakthrough
roildiminish
roil: standard unit headword | diminish: standard unit headword
#8 Sentence Equivalence
Publicity around the celebrities’ donations to charity is often greeted with cynicism, but a study of the celebrities’ donation shows that they do ______ other donation.Publicity around the celebrities’ donations to charity is often greeted with cynicism, but a study of the celebrities’ donation shows that they do ______ other donation.
答案：C, D　draw; elicit
bargreet
bar: standard unit headword | greet: standard unit headword
#9 SE
Primates are, for the most part, very __________: walk through a tropical forest, and it is highly likely that if you encounter any mammals, it will be a primate that you see or hear.Primates are, for the most part, very __________: walk through a tropical forest, and it is highly likely that if you encounter any mammals, it will be a primate that you see or hear.
答案：B, D　noticeable; conspicuous
noticeableprimate
noticeable: standard unit headword | primate: standard unit headword
#10 TC DOUBLE
In a 1952 essay, Thomas Kuhn argued that Robert Boyle’s corpuscular philosophy was purely mechanistic. Kuhn, however, is not (i)_______ in attributing a strict Cartesian mechanism to Boyle. In fact, this view of Boyle (ii)_______ Fontenelle, who, in his History of the Royal Academy of Sciences of 1733, identifies corpuscularism with Cartesian mechanism.In a 1952 essay, Thomas Kuhn argued that Robert Boyle’s corpuscular philosophy was purely mechanistic. Kuhn, however, is not (i)_______ in attributing a strict Cartesian mechanism to Boyle. In fact, this view of Boyle (ii)_______ Fontenelle, who, in his History of the Royal Academy of Sciences of 1733, identifies corpuscularism with Cartesian mechanism.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, E　unique; can be traced to
mechanisticjustify
mechanistic: standard unit headword | justify: standard unit headword
#11 Text Completion
Logically, one might assume that the nostalgia in which Guzman engages at times in his documentary about Chilean history would be (i)______ critical reflection and thus leave the film intellectually flaccid. This conclusion would be (ii)______, however, insofar as not all nostalgia necessarily results in a (iii)______ of critical thought.Logically, one might assume that the nostalgia in which Guzman engages at times in his documentary about Chilean history would be (i)______ critical reflection and thus leave the film intellectually flaccid. This conclusion would be (ii)______, however, insofar as not all nostalgia necessarily results in a (iii)______ of critical thought.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, I　an impediment to; hasty; stagnation
warrantdocumentary
warrant: standard unit headword | documentary: standard unit headword

In her career as an editor, she pruned and shaped many a writer’s ______ prose into crisp lucidity.In her career as an editor, she pruned and shaped many a writer’s ______ prose into crisp lucidity.
答案：A, C　wayward; errant
urbaneerrant
urbane: standard unit headword | errant: standard unit headword

答案：D, F　a difficult; an enigmatic
qualitywhimsical

Blank 3

dourtepid

#16 Text Completion
The usual (i)______ spending public monies on scientific projects is that such projects have the potential to make our lives healthier, safer, and more productive. However, the fact that science—even "pure" science—can strengthen democracy and promote public participation in the political process is hardly ever (ii)______. It should be that scientific literacy (iii)______ democracy, and this is an important ancillary benefit of the promotion of science.The usual (i)______ spending public monies on scientific projects is that such projects have the potential to make our lives healthier, safer, and more productive. However, the fact that science—even "pure" science—can strengthen democracy and promote public participation in the political process is hardly ever (ii)______. It should be that scientific literacy (iii)______ democracy, and this is an important ancillary benefit of the promotion of science.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, E, H　rationale for; mentioned; energizes
energizerationale
energize: standard unit headword | rationale: standard unit headword
#17 TC SINGLE
Needing to conceal their increasing alienation from each other, the family settled into what amounted to voluble nonsense, in that they would ____________ but seldom communicate.Needing to conceal their increasing alienation from each other, the family settled into what amounted to voluble nonsense, in that they would ____________ but seldom communicate.
答案：C　natter
voluble
voluble: standard unit headword
#18 TC TRIPLE
Letters present an awkward stumbling block to those who take a high line about literature as a custodian of moral values. The letters of accomplished poets and novelists are - as often as not - about drinking and infidelity, about sponging and wasting time. Consequently, one's (i) __________ the writer as (ii) __________ can often come in for a rude shock on contemplation of the writer's correspondence: the soul that composed the poems and the self that wrote the letters can seem (iii) __________.Letters present an awkward stumbling block to those who take a high line about literature as a custodian of moral values. The letters of accomplished poets and novelists are - as often as not - about drinking and infidelity, about sponging and wasting time. Consequently, one's (i) __________ the writer as (ii) __________ can often come in for a rude shock on contemplation of the writer's correspondence: the soul that composed the poems and the self that wrote the letters can seem (iii) __________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, E, G　reverence for; exemplary; irreconcilable
accomplished
accomplished: standard unit headword
#19 Text Completion
The paleontologist examined the problem afresh, believing that the accepted classification ______ the essential continuity of the specimens by making specious distinctions among them.The paleontologist examined the problem afresh, believing that the accepted classification ______ the essential continuity of the specimens by making specious distinctions among them.
答案：B　belied
belieafresh
belie: standard unit headword | afresh: standard unit headword
#20 TC DOUBLE
After peaking around 3.5 million years ago, the number of large mammalian carnivore species declined gradually over the next million and a half years or so, mostly because the rate at which new species (i) ____________ slowed down while the extinction rate (ii) ____________.After peaking around 3.5 million years ago, the number of large mammalian carnivore species declined gradually over the next million and a half years or so, mostly because the rate at which new species (i) ____________ slowed down while the extinction rate (ii) ____________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, E　originated; remained largely unchanged
carnivore
carnivore: standard unit headword

Though Blandford's manifesto envisions the victory of his political philosophy as __________, Dreyer denies that the manifesto is a deterministic document.Though Blandford's manifesto envisions the victory of his political philosophy as __________, Dreyer denies that the manifesto is a deterministic document.
答案：E　inevitable
document
document: standard unit headword
#22 Text Completion
The modest but functional new wing finally gives the museum the ______ to serve its visitors properly, including multiple entrances to eliminate the lines that used to snake around the building.The modest but functional new wing finally gives the museum the ______ to serve its visitors properly, including multiple entrances to eliminate the lines that used to snake around the building.
答案：B　wherewithal
functionalwherewithal

Usually data are compiled, a hypothesis is tested, and conclusions might then become news if deemed solid. Because Francis's idea links climate change to severe weather, it resonated immediately with the media and the public, and Francis has been forced to (ii) ____________ before the scientific process has been (iii) ____________.Precipitate interest in Jennifer Francis's theory that anomalously rapid warming of the Arctic disrupts weather patterns in temperate latitudes has (i) ____________ the normal life cycle of a scientific controversy. Usually data are compiled, a hypothesis is tested, and conclusions might then become news if deemed solid. Because Francis's idea links climate change to severe weather, it resonated immediately with the media and the public, and Francis has been forced to (ii) ____________ before the scientific process has been (iii) ____________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, I　inverted; defend her findings; concluded
qualify
qualify: standard unit headword

The of the folktales in this collection does not inhibit the conveyance of important lessons; indeed, the didactic effect would be lost if the stories were more protracted.The of the folktales in this collection does not inhibit the conveyance of important lessons; indeed, the didactic effect would be lost if the stories were more protracted.
答案：D, F　brevity; economy
didactic

答案：B　interrogated

Blank 2

Although Dostoyevsky was not the (i) __________ portrayed by some biographers, at least implicitly guilty of the terrible crimes he depicted in his novels, he was notoriously (ii) __________. Indeed, his attitude toward people who questioned him about his work was anything but friendly. No such problems bother students of Chekhov. On the contrary, it is difficult to think of a writer of equal fame and importance who, on close inspection, proves to be such a (iii) __________ human being.Although Dostoyevsky was not the (i) __________ portrayed by some biographers, at least implicitly guilty of the terrible crimes he depicted in his novels, he was notoriously (ii) __________. Indeed, his attitude toward people who questioned him about his work was anything but friendly. No such problems bother students of Chekhov. On the contrary, it is difficult to think of a writer of equal fame and importance who, on close inspection, proves to be such a (iii) __________ human being.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, F, G　monster; prickly; sympathetic
pedantnovel
pedant: standard unit headword | novel: standard unit headword
#28 Sentence Equivalence
Circulatory systems on organisms originated in widely separated epochs, according to the fossil record, and under a broad range of circumstances; the myriad forms they take attest to that ______ of origin.Circulatory systems on organisms originated in widely separated epochs, according to the fossil record, and under a broad range of circumstances; the myriad forms they take attest to that ______ of origin.
答案：D, F　heterogeneity; diversity
myriad
myriad: standard unit headword
#29 TC SINGLE
Despite the fact that some of the company's hiring and promotion practices have been widely __________, these practices still persist in certain divisions of the company.Despite the fact that some of the company's hiring and promotion practices have been widely __________, these practices still persist in certain divisions of the company.
答案：C　proscribed
proscribe
proscribe: standard unit headword
#30 SE
Our mass media are much more fascinated by bad ideas or the failure of good ones than by successes: we drown in bad news--tales of how things went wrong-and, consequently, we are lacking in any _________ discussion on how they might go right.Our mass media are much more fascinated by bad ideas or the failure of good ones than by successes: we drown in bad news--tales of how things went wrong-and, consequently, we are lacking in any _________ discussion on how they might go right.
答案：A, C　incisive; trenchant
tantalize
tantalize: standard unit headword

Although evolutionary psychologists do not seem quite as imperialist in their intellectual ambitions as their sociobiologist forebears of the 1970s, they tend, in some critics’ view, to be no less ______ in their claims.Although evolutionary psychologists do not seem quite as imperialist in their intellectual ambitions as their sociobiologist forebears of the 1970s, they tend, in some critics’ view, to be no less ______ in their claims.
答案：B, D　arrogant; hubristic
imperialist

Anna Barbauld's 1804 edition of Richardson's correspondence has been (i) __________. Although she (ii) __________ many of the letters that she included and made stylistic changes throughout, often for the worse, she was, by the standards of her time, quite (iii) __________ editor with regard to her respect for the original texts.Anna Barbauld's 1804 edition of Richardson's correspondence has been (i) __________. Although she (ii) __________ many of the letters that she included and made stylistic changes throughout, often for the worse, she was, by the standards of her time, quite (iii) __________ editor with regard to her respect for the original texts.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, F, G　unjustly disparaged; abridged; a scrupulous
scrupulous
scrupulous: standard unit headword

According to the ancient Chinese philosopher Mozi, the world's problems stem from ____________: rather than desiring what is best for the community, people care most about themselves, then their family and friends, and little about others.According to the ancient Chinese philosopher Mozi, the world's problems stem from ____________: rather than desiring what is best for the community, people care most about themselves, then their family and friends, and little about others.
答案：E　partiality
hubris

答案：C, E　itinerant; peripatetic
quixotic
quixotic: standard unit headword

Blank 2
答案：C, E　credulity; gullible
gullible

imperial

答案：C　prudent
flagrant

答案：A, F　consequence; result

His anecdotes seem (ii) __________ but when carefully scrutinized, their accuracy is less than impressive. What rings false is his picture of the impresarios as unerring Machiavellian schemers-villains who never (iii) __________ in their efforts to pile up a fortune and ruin the world of music. However, my experience in the concert and recording world suggests that there is more bumbling than sinister plotting.Lebrecht recounts many entertaining scandals, (i) __________ himself on some points in deference to the laws of libel. His anecdotes seem (ii) __________ but when carefully scrutinized, their accuracy is less than impressive. What rings false is his picture of the impresarios as unerring Machiavellian schemers-villains who never (iii) __________ in their efforts to pile up a fortune and ruin the world of music. However, my experience in the concert and recording world suggests that there is more bumbling than sinister plotting.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, E, H　restraining; plausible; blunder

#40 Text Completion
Environmental models—mathematical representations designed to simulate natural systems—are regularly used by litigants in legal disputes over environmental issues. Unfortunately, the (i)______ scientific model is (ii)______ in environmental tort litigation. Because of the adversarial nature of litigation, models are often used by one side to (iii)______ empirical evidence presented by the other. And because modeling is a particularly technical field, the task of assessing a given model’s relevance and reliability may exceed the abilities of judges and juries.Environmental models—mathematical representations designed to simulate natural systems—are regularly used by litigants in legal disputes over environmental issues. Unfortunately, the (i)______ scientific model is (ii)______ in environmental tort litigation. Because of the adversarial nature of litigation, models are often used by one side to (iii)______ empirical evidence presented by the other. And because modeling is a particularly technical field, the task of assessing a given model’s relevance and reliability may exceed the abilities of judges and juries.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, F, H　uncertainty inherent in; exacerbated; obfuscate
issuelitigation
issue: standard unit headword | litigation: standard unit headword

The Chavez Pass archaeological site was initially interpreted as indicative of ______ society, since it was thought to have been at the center of a cluster of smaller, contemporary settlements that it presumably controlled.The Chavez Pass archaeological site was initially interpreted as indicative of ______ society, since it was thought to have been at the center of a cluster of smaller, contemporary settlements that it presumably controlled.
答案：B, F　a hierarchical; a stratified

答案：B, D　precarious; perilous
covert

答案：B, F　collective; social

Blank 2
Blank 3

polish

Blank 2

#50 Sentence Equivalence
An abundance of nutrient-rich pollution in estuaries causes algae to ______, much as houseplants grow better when their soil contains added fertilizer.An abundance of nutrient-rich pollution in estuaries causes algae to ______, much as houseplants grow better when their soil contains added fertilizer.
答案：A, B　abound; proliferate
stagnate
stagnate: standard unit headword
#51 Text Completion
Some novelists immodestly idealized and exaggerate the significance of their work, but others, ______ to exalt the role of the writer, question a transcendent view of the art.Some novelists immodestly idealized and exaggerate the significance of their work, but others, ______ to exalt the role of the writer, question a transcendent view of the art.
答案：B　declining
exalt
exalt: standard unit headword
#52 SE
Modern historians' silence on British views of French empire-building is perhaps explained, at least in part, by the __________ of Victorian-era scholarship on pre-1870 French colonial enterprise.Modern historians' silence on British views of French empire-building is perhaps explained, at least in part, by the __________ of Victorian-era scholarship on pre-1870 French colonial enterprise.
答案：A, F　paucity; scarcity
scarcity
scarcity: standard unit headword
#53 Sentence Equivalence

答案：C, D　respite; relief

The only (iii)______ sensory deprivation of the sessions came from the handsome color slides favored by the corporate presenters.Despite the occasional (i)______ of their venues, the culture of corporate conferences is a deeply (ii)______ one, each day consisted of nearly nine hours of continuous lectures and panels enlivened by pleasantries or anything that could be construed as a joke. The only (iii)______ sensory deprivation of the sessions came from the handsome color slides favored by the corporate presenters.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, E, I　opulence; ascetic; respite from
pleasantry

#55 SE

答案：B, F　supplant; replace

答案：B, F　perspicacity; discernment

Blank 2

He is among the most uninhibited comic performers around, but here he buttons his lip and stares straight ahead. Perhaps without quite knowing it, the audience waits for a wink, a hint that some of the (ii)______ spirit that animated his previous movies might be lurking inside the (iii)______ manner he presents in this film.His new role gives the normally clownish actor a chance to impress audiences with his (i)______ . He is among the most uninhibited comic performers around, but here he buttons his lip and stares straight ahead. Perhaps without quite knowing it, the audience waits for a wink, a hint that some of the (ii)______ spirit that animated his previous movies might be lurking inside the (iii)______ manner he presents in this film.

Blank 2
Blank 3

答案：C, E　unabated; undiminished

Was ever a bookish soul so cracklingly unmediated, so (ii)______ raw life? He was as vivid physically as he was mentally, almost perversely alert, completely at home in the world of matter, repulsed by (iii)______.Saul’s particular combination of intellectuality and vitality was not paradoxical; it was category shattering. (i)______ was, in a way, his very theme. Was ever a bookish soul so cracklingly unmediated, so (ii)______ raw life? 答案：A, D　preachy; sanctimonious

Specifically, the pulsar’s periodic radiation pattern was initially interpreted as (iii)______ rather than—as it turned out to be—the natural product of the spinning of a neutron star.When pulsars were first discovered, some of the astrophysicists involved briefly (i)______ these types of stars might be (ii)______ extraterrestrial intelligence. As theories, quantum field theory and theory of general relatively have been astonishingly (i)______ that no experiment yet has shown a crack in either of them, even though both of them (ii)______ wildly counterintuitive properties in physical reality. And yet even proponents note that the two theories are (iii)______ , a fact that has disallowed theorists—at least so far—from incorporating both into a self-consistent theory of quantum gravity.As theories, quantum field theory and theory of general relatively have been astonishingly (i)______ that no experiment yet has shown a crack in either of them, even though both of them (ii)______ wildly counterintuitive properties in physical reality. And yet even proponents note that the two theories are (iii)______ , a fact that has disallowed theorists—at least so far—from incorporating both into a self-consistent theory of quantum gravity.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, F, H　successful; posit; deeply at odds with each

Archeologists have shown, for example, that the largest contiguous tract of what was thought to be virgin rain forest in the southern Amazon had been transformed into a cultural parkland before European contact, and many of the forest islands in West Africa’s savanna-forest transition zone are (ii)______ as well.Recent scholarship has questioned the (i)______ of tropical forests around the world. Archeologists have shown, for example, that the largest contiguous tract of what was thought to be virgin rain forest in the southern Amazon had been transformed into a cultural parkland before European contact, and many of the forest islands in West Africa’s savanna-forest transition zone are (ii)______ as well.

Blank 2

But a wire and a few resistors may (ii)______ a message as securely, according to a physicist who claims to have devised a simple and uncrackable scheme. The idea shows that more (iii)______ methods might compete with budding quantum cryptography.With the grand ambition of sending unbreakable coded messages, some physicists are using exotic tools—quantum mechanics and streams of individual photos—to shut out (i)______. But a wire and a few resistors may (ii)______ a message as securely, according to a physicist who claims to have devised a simple and uncrackable scheme. Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, G　prying eyes; convey; mundane

But in the1990s a small British theater company, Shared Experience, began to (iii) the received wisdom about adaptations by developing thrilling and uniquely theatrical versions of several classic novels.Famous books have been(i) the theater: Dickens suffered so much hackneyed staging that he felt forced to change his writing style, throwing time shifts into his narratives to trip up the adapters. While many tools can automatically locate patterns in large data sets, they cannot yet distinguish truly significant patterns from those that are (i) __________. Thus statisticians, who by virtue of their training are (ii) __________ identifying (iii) __________ interpretations of patterns in data, must increasingly bear the burden of scrutinizing data and challenging conclusions based on statistical flukes.While many tools can automatically locate patterns in large data sets, they cannot yet distinguish truly significant patterns from those that are (i) __________. Thus statisticians, who by virtue of their training are (ii) __________ identifying (iii) __________ interpretations of patterns in data, must increasingly bear the burden of scrutinizing data and challenging conclusions based on statistical flukes.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, E, G　merely fortuitous; adept at; spurious
spurious
spurious: standard unit headword
#103 SE
This novel is not great literature; there are too many characters, too many subplots, and no clearly defined protagonist, making for ___________ story.This novel is not great literature; there are too many characters, too many subplots, and no clearly defined protagonist, making for ___________ story.
答案：B, E　a diffuse; an amorphous
novel
novel: standard unit headword
#104 TC DOUBLE
The lecturer (i) to provide innovative insights into important issues, but her promise of originality was more than she delivered: most of her pronouncements were (ii) .The lecturer (i) to provide innovative insights into important issues, but her promise of originality was more than she delivered: most of her pronouncements were (ii) .
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, D　undertook; platitudinous
issue
issue: standard unit headword
#105 TC TRIPLE
Unlike news reports, theater isn’t expected to stick to the facts. Built on a sandy foundation of make-believe and (i)_______, the form, by nature traffics in (ii)_______. Good documentary drama exploits its inherent paradox: creating artifice from verbatim texts, it uncovers truths by playing on the tension between what’s real and what’s (iii)_______.Unlike news reports, theater isn’t expected to stick to the facts. Built on a sandy foundation of make-believe and (i)_______, the form, by nature traffics in (ii)_______. Good documentary drama exploits its inherent paradox: creating artifice from verbatim texts, it uncovers truths by playing on the tension between what’s real and what’s (iii)_______.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, H　pretense; illusion; invented
documentary
documentary: standard unit headword
#106 TC DOUBLE
Adam Smith's defense of capitalism was (i) __________ but (ii) __________: Smith strongly endorsed it over all previous socioeconomic systems as the one in which the most people possible could live decent lives, yet he was not the dogmatic advocate of free enterprise that he is so often thought to have been.Adam Smith's defense of capitalism was (i) __________ but (ii) __________: Smith strongly endorsed it over all previous socioeconomic systems as the one in which the most people possible could live decent lives, yet he was not the dogmatic advocate of free enterprise that he is so often thought to have been.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, F　resolute; qualified
qualify
qualify: standard unit headword
#107 TC SINGLE
It would be easy to nitpick about the historical details that this novel has gotten laughably wrong, but ultimately its failings are not factual but __________: matters of pacing, dialogue, composition.It would be easy to nitpick about the historical details that this novel has gotten laughably wrong, but ultimately its failings are not factual but __________: matters of pacing, dialogue, composition.
答案：C　formal
novel
novel: standard unit headword
#108 SE
Julia's success as an academic scientist came largely as the product of her gift for the scholastic _________: although definitely not a genius, she had a plodding tenacity and an apparent immunity to drudgery.Julia's success as an academic scientist came largely as the product of her gift for the scholastic _________: although definitely not a genius, she had a plodding tenacity and an apparent immunity to drudgery.
答案：A, F　routine; grind
apparent
apparent: standard unit headword
#109 SE
The impact of the film on people's perceptions of what happened illustrates the power of fiction to history: patently inaccurate, the story told by the film has nevertheless come to be the standard account.The impact of the film on people's perceptions of what happened illustrates the power of fiction to history: patently inaccurate, the story told by the film has nevertheless come to be the standard account.
答案：A, D　trump; supplant
supplant
supplant: standard unit headword

Analyses of Tracy K. Smith’s poetry that (i)_________ its formal and aesthetic qualities are (ii)________, as Smith’s formal choices are ultimately (iii)_______ her political arguments.Analyses of Tracy K. Smith’s poetry that (i)_________ its formal and aesthetic qualities are (ii)________, as Smith’s formal choices are ultimately (iii)_______ her political arguments.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, F, G　prioritize; deficient; ancillary to
quality
quality: standard unit headword
#111 TC SINGLE
In early twentieth-century India, the claim that films from the West screened by "first-class theaters" were exclusively "high-class features" was __________, since a film's being screened by such theaters was interpreted as a confirmation of quality.In early twentieth-century India, the claim that films from the West screened by "first-class theaters" were exclusively "high-class features" was __________, since a film's being screened by such theaters was interpreted as a confirmation of quality.
答案：D　tautological
quality
quality: standard unit headword
#112 TC DOUBLE
Scottish writer Mary Brunton's novels Self-control (1811) and Discipline (1814) are undeniably (i)_________: her plots, involving virtue rewarded and excess avoided, (ii)_________ cautionary tales, though the psychological analysis that pervades the books saves them from blandness and sentimentality.Scottish writer Mary Brunton's novels Self-control (1811) and Discipline (1814) are undeniably (i)_________: her plots, involving virtue rewarded and excess avoided, (ii)_________ cautionary tales, though the psychological analysis that pervades the books saves them from blandness and sentimentality.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, D　didactic; amount to
noveldidactic
novel: standard unit headword | didactic: standard unit headword
#113 TC TRIPLE
For scientists who wish to conduct research in the national parks, the new plan promises a warm welcome in place of the (i)_______, or even resistance, that often greets requests for research permits. The plan says that “parks can and should be centers for broad scientific research and inquiry.” It also maintains that scientific research should be (ii)_______ in parks where it can be performed without (iii)_______ other park values.For scientists who wish to conduct research in the national parks, the new plan promises a warm welcome in place of the (i)_______, or even resistance, that often greets requests for research permits. The plan says that “parks can and should be centers for broad scientific research and inquiry.” It also maintains that scientific research should be (ii)_______ in parks where it can be performed without (iii)_______ other park values.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, F, H　indifference; facilitated; jeopardizing
greet
greet: standard unit headword
#114 TC SINGLE
Garte claims that, as a species, humans are __________ certain types of air pollutants; indeed, humans have been known to survive relatively well in regions where other species have died off because of poor air quality.Garte claims that, as a species, humans are __________ certain types of air pollutants; indeed, humans have been known to survive relatively well in regions where other species have died off because of poor air quality.
答案：C　insensitive to
quality
quality: standard unit headword
#115 SE
Even though Our Lady of the Forest achieves clinical evenhandedness in depicting the most depressing members of a small logging town, the novel lacks a single likable character and too often runs aground on __________ writing.Even though Our Lady of the Forest achieves clinical evenhandedness in depicting the most depressing members of a small logging town, the novel lacks a single likable character and too often runs aground on __________ writing.
答案：A, D　stilted; wooden
novel
novel: standard unit headword
#116 TC DOUBLE
Prior to the advent of the scientific method, many were skeptical of experimentation as a means for seeking knowledge. It was widely thought that(i) could only produce(ii) findings.Prior to the advent of the scientific method, many were skeptical of experimentation as a means for seeking knowledge. It was widely thought that(i) could only produce(ii) findings.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, D　artificially produced experiences; spurious

#117 TC TRIPLE
Climate science journals are often too hermetic for even the most (i)_______ layperson. The gravity of the issues discussed requires that we (ii)_______ such (iii)_______: we need publications that engage the broader public in discussions of how we interact with the natural world.Climate science journals are often too hermetic for even the most (i)_______ layperson. The gravity of the issues discussed requires that we (ii)_______ such (iii)_______: we need publications that engage the broader public in discussions of how we interact with the natural world.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, G　educated; abandon; academic insularity

issue: standard unit headword
#118 TC SINGLE
The efficacy of a placebo may not ___________ deception: inert sugar pills have been shown to reduce the symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome even in patients who were explicitly told they were receiving a placebo.The efficacy of a placebo may not ___________ deception: inert sugar pills have been shown to reduce the symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome even in patients who were explicitly told they were receiving a placebo.
答案：C　require
justify
justify: standard unit headword
#119 SE
Although it is premature to say whether nanomotors would __________ conventional methods, Wang believes that the most promising applications for nanomotors lie in fast drug delivery or low-cost cleanup of environmental pollutants.Although it is premature to say whether nanomotors would __________ conventional methods, Wang believes that the most promising applications for nanomotors lie in fast drug delivery or low-cost cleanup of environmental pollutants.
答案：D, E　supplant; supersede
supplant
supplant: standard unit headword
#120 TC DOUBLE
When the columnist wrote about controversial issues, her own position was not always (i) __________ her analysis of the arguments being put forth on either side. If she (ii) __________ the reasoning of proponents of one side, that did not mean that she rejected their position.When the columnist wrote about controversial issues, her own position was not always (i) __________ her analysis of the arguments being put forth on either side. If she (ii) __________ the reasoning of proponents of one side, that did not mean that she rejected their position.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, F　inferable from; challenged

One contemporary observed the (ii) __________ of the painter to his social milieu: its meretricious taste was well served by his (iii) __________.The artist's portraits of the social elite, with their luscious sensuality and insistent prettiness, have often been regarded with sly amusement, if not outright (i) __________. One contemporary observed the (ii) __________ of the painter to his social milieu: its meretricious taste was well served by his (iii) __________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, F, H　derision; appositeness; willingness to flatter

milieu: standard unit headword

During winter, the nearshore waters in the northwestern Gulf of Mexico can become quite _______: storms carry silt in from land and stir up the mud of the seafloor.During winter, the nearshore waters in the northwestern Gulf of Mexico can become quite _______: storms carry silt in from land and stir up the mud of the seafloor.
答案：B, F　turbid; roiled

. The fact that all major planets have satellites was __________ before the space age; however, the numbers of known satellites for these planets have been greatly expanded by spacecraft observations.. The fact that all major planets have satellites was __________ before the space age; however, the numbers of known satellites for these planets have been greatly expanded by spacecraft observations.
答案：B　recognized

Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, F　undermine; camouflage

Why Zona Gale - a Pulitzer Prize-winning writer whose novels were both a critical and popular success - has fallen so far out of the literary canon is the fascinating subject of Deborah Lindsay Williams' recent book. Williams concludes that Gale's embrace of literary (i) __________ accounts for her eventual decline in reputation. Gale sought professional affiliation with other women writers of many stripes, and Williams contends that she has therefore been (ii) __________ by a later literary paradigm that favors (iii) __________ as a marker of artistic prowess.Why Zona Gale - a Pulitzer Prize-winning writer whose novels were both a critical and popular success - has fallen so far out of the literary canon is the fascinating subject of Deborah Lindsay Williams' recent book. Williams concludes that Gale's embrace of literary (i) __________ accounts for her eventual decline in reputation. Gale sought professional affiliation with other women writers of many stripes, and Williams contends that she has therefore been (ii) __________ by a later literary paradigm that favors (iii) __________ as a marker of artistic prowess.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, I　sisterhood; marginalized; individual exceptionalism

答案：A, D　trump; supplant

Wild-eyed and boisterous, she sought out the companionship of others, even as her face revealed a great (ii) .As her fortunes diminished and her reputation ebbed, Hopkins' need for (i) increased. Wild-eyed and boisterous, she sought out the companionship of others, even as her face revealed a great (ii) .

Famous books have been (i) __________ the theater: Dickens suffered so much hackneyed staging that he felt forced to change his writing style, throwing time shifts into his narratives to trip up the adapters. Undeterred, theatrical managements ever since have used other people's literary repute to fill seats, though the adaptations still seldom (ii) __________ their literary models. But in the 1990s a small British theater company, Shared Experience, began to (iii) __________ the received wisdom about adaptations by developing thrilling and uniquely theatrical versions of several classic novels.Famous books have been (i) __________ the theater: Dickens suffered so much hackneyed staging that he felt forced to change his writing style, throwing time shifts into his narratives to trip up the adapters. Undeterred, theatrical managements ever since have used other people's literary repute to fill seats, though the adaptations still seldom (ii) __________ their literary models. But in the 1990s a small British theater company, Shared Experience, began to (iii) __________ the received wisdom about adaptations by developing thrilling and uniquely theatrical versions of several classic novels.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, D, G　much abused by; equaled; confound

The system of thought approached a fundamentally __________ domain as though it had a unitary essence, and the system was therefore inevitably in error.The system of thought approached a fundamentally __________ domain as though it had a unitary essence, and the system was therefore inevitably in error.
答案：A　heterogeneous

答案：B, D　shortcoming; fault

China has a long tradition of __________ the written word; whereas in Renaissance Europe, painting, sculpture, and architecture were viewed as the apex of artistic achievement, in China calligraphy was the preeminent art form.China has a long tradition of __________ the written word; whereas in Renaissance Europe, painting, sculpture, and architecture were viewed as the apex of artistic achievement, in China calligraphy was the preeminent art form.
答案：B, F　venerating; exalting

Nostalgia is to history as celebrity is to fame. Both nostalgia and celebrity are ways for the observer to (i) things that are exotic, and that are characterized by their (ii) the ordinary, and to make them, instead, familiar matters upon which the observer’s own feelings can feed.Nostalgia is to history as celebrity is to fame. Both nostalgia and celebrity are ways for the observer to (i) things that are exotic, and that are characterized by their (ii) the ordinary, and to make them, instead, familiar matters upon which the observer’s own feelings can feed.
Blank 1
Blank 2

The Wildensteins, for instance, were far more successful financially, but they were (iii) __________ beyond measure.As a consequence of his need for (i) __________ and his (ii) __________ self-advertisement, Joseph Duveen's career is better known than those of most of his fellow dealers of museum-quality material. The Wildensteins, for instance, were far more successful financially, but they were (iii) __________ beyond measure.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3

答案：C, D　novel; original

答案：A, C　sparse; paltry

The superficial agreement that characterizes the books' accounts of the incident is (i)________, since it obscures the profound (ii)________ that exist in their authors' understandings of the incident’s significance.The superficial agreement that characterizes the books' accounts of the incident is (i)________, since it obscures the profound (ii)________ that exist in their authors' understandings of the incident’s significance.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, D　misleading; differences

答案：D, F　spurious; specious
spurious
spurious: standard unit headword
#143 TC TRIPLE
The author's new novel is everything that her first was not: obvious and (i) __________ rather than understated and subtly shaded. Instead of quietly revealing themselves through tiny, unexpected gestures, the characters are forced to make (ii) __________ statements that announce exactly what they are thinking and serve primarily to underscore the author's sociological points. There are occasional glimpses of the (iii) __________ and emotional wisdom so lavishly demonstrated in the author's previous novel--gifts, the reader hopes, that will be more fruitfully employed in her next.The author's new novel is everything that her first was not: obvious and (i) __________ rather than understated and subtly shaded. Instead of quietly revealing themselves through tiny, unexpected gestures, the characters are forced to make (ii) __________ statements that announce exactly what they are thinking and serve primarily to underscore the author's sociological points. There are occasional glimpses of the (iii) __________ and emotional wisdom so lavishly demonstrated in the author's previous novel--gifts, the reader hopes, that will be more fruitfully employed in her next.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, F, I　melodramatic; tendentious; psychological acuity

novel: standard unit headword

In his role as a librarian and historian, Ama Bontemps was a great________ and a preserver of the documents of Black cultural heritage.In his role as a librarian and historian, Ama Bontemps was a great________ and a preserver of the documents of Black cultural heritage.
答案：A, C　annalist; chronicler

Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, F　complicated; recover

答案：C, D　quixotic; idealistic

There's ___________ throughout the Broadway adaptation of The Color Purple that, while hardly true to the harrowing bleakness of the early chapters of Alice Walker's novel, does bring to mind the enjoyably hokey cinematic lavishness of the film version.There's ___________ throughout the Broadway adaptation of The Color Purple that, while hardly true to the harrowing bleakness of the early chapters of Alice Walker's novel, does bring to mind the enjoyably hokey cinematic lavishness of the film version.
答案：B, D　a splendor; a sumptuousness

答案：A, D　plausible; conceivable

Science is so good at what it does that many people feel a (i) __________ to apply it to social and political issues, yet experience shows that science may add little to or even (ii) __________ social and political questions.Science is so good at what it does that many people feel a (i) __________ to apply it to social and political issues, yet experience shows that science may add little to or even (ii) __________ social and political questions.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, E　temptation; occlude

Every natural language has redundancy built in; this is why people can understand text (iii) __________ and why they can understand conversation in a noisy room.Although often seen as simply an inefficiency, redundancy in language actually serves as the (i) __________ to (ii) __________. It provides second chances, in other words. Every natural language has redundancy built in; this is why people can understand text (iii) __________ and why they can understand conversation in a noisy room.

Blank 2
Blank 3

答案：C, D　belied; contradicted

The scholarly quality of the work is (ii) __________ on every page, and yet it is not a dry, specialist academic tome.Wald's decision to tell Rosetta Tharpe's story to a wider audience in Shout, Sister, Shout! (i) __________ the rigor of the book's scholarship or the acuity of its theory. The scholarly quality of the work is (ii) __________ on every page, and yet it is not a dry, specialist academic tome.

Blank 2
答案：B, D　does not compromise; evident

In 1998, observations of a (i) __________ in the growth of atmospheric methane, a potent greenhouse gas, suggested that many of the scenarios for global warming provided in a 1995 assessment may have been overly (ii) __________. Celebration was, however, premature: subsequent research has established that the observed phenomena were likely caused by (iii) ___________ declines in industrial and natural emissions.In 1998, observations of a (i) __________ in the growth of atmospheric methane, a potent greenhouse gas, suggested that many of the scenarios for global warming provided in a 1995 assessment may have been overly (ii) __________. Celebration was, however, premature: subsequent research has established that the observed phenomena were likely caused by (iii) ___________ declines in industrial and natural emissions.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, I　slowdown; gloomy; temporary

答案：B, C　abstraction; generality

答案：E, F　workaday; quotidian

#157 TC DOUBLE
Most scientists would argue that consciousness is (i) __________ animals with relatively complex brains. The more scientists study animal behavior and brain anatomy, however, the more it seems that very complex brains are (ii) __________ consciousness.Most scientists would argue that consciousness is (i) __________ animals with relatively complex brains. The more scientists study animal behavior and brain anatomy, however, the more it seems that very complex brains are (ii) __________ consciousness.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, E　restricted to; unnecessary for
document
document: standard unit headword

Blank 2
答案：A, D　imagined; defensible

It is hardly surprising that technological fixes appear to be, more than ever, the best solution to minimize future rise of atmospheric temperatures. Unfortunately, discussion of such solutions is marked by (i) rather than (ii) . That remains so even after excluding certain utopian infatuations with those renewable conversions whose limited, exceedingly diffuse, or hard-to-capture resources (be they jet stream winds or ocean waves) (ii) their becoming energy sources during the next few decades.It is hardly surprising that technological fixes appear to be, more than ever, the best solution to minimize future rise of atmospheric temperatures. Unfortunately, discussion of such solutions is marked by (i) rather than (ii) . That remains so even after excluding certain utopian infatuations with those renewable conversions whose limited, exceedingly diffuse, or hard-to-capture resources (be they jet stream winds or ocean waves) (ii) their becoming energy sources during the next few decades.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, G　exaggerated expectations; realism; preclude

In his discussion of art, Ramachandran makes no distinction between the arousal value of a stimulus and its aesthetic value; he takes the emotional power of an artwork to be __________ that work's aesthetic quality.In his discussion of art, Ramachandran makes no distinction between the arousal value of a stimulus and its aesthetic value; he takes the emotional power of an artwork to be __________ that work's aesthetic quality.
答案：B, E　tantamount to; commensurate with

The author is adept at dealing with trivial details in the poets he studies, but he is quite (ii) __________ when it comes to illuminating the overall vision. The verbal intricacy and panache of his writing contrast with its conceptual (iii) __________. If there are very few authentic ideas in the poems under consideration, there is also a shortage of them in the author's commentary.Perhaps the best one can say of this new book of criticism is that it is scintillatingly (i) __________. The author is adept at dealing with trivial details in the poets he studies, but he is quite (ii) __________ when it comes to illuminating the overall vision. If there are very few authentic ideas in the poems under consideration, there is also a shortage of them in the author's commentary.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, I　shallow; indifferent; poverty

Acertain amount of theoretical frenzy about comics today is (i)______ . After all, similar frenzies have been in other art forms in periods of their rapid development, for instance, the debates about painting that roiled Renaissance Italy. But such intellectual (ii)______ rarely precedes creative glory. On the contrary, it commonly indicates that an artistic (iii)______ , having been made and recognized, is over, and that a process of increasing strained emulation and diminishing returns has setin.Acertain amount of theoretical frenzy about comics today is (i)______ . After all, similar frenzies have been in other art forms in periods of their rapid development, for instance, the debates about painting that roiled Renaissance Italy. But such intellectual (ii)______ rarely precedes creative glory. Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, F, I　understandable; arousal; breakthrough

答案：A, C　baseless; untenable

#212 Text Completion
In adolescence, (i)______ interactions are crucial in forging a self-identity. To be sure, this process often plays out in (ii)______ as a means of defining and shoring up the sense of self. Kids will seek out like-minded companions, and spurn others who seem different. But when kept within reasonable bounds, this in-group (iii)______ generally evolves into a more mature friendship pattern.In adolescence, (i)______ interactions are crucial in forging a self-identity. To be sure, this process often plays out in (ii)______ as a means of defining and shoring up the sense of self. Kids will seek out like-minded companions, and spurn others who seem different. But when kept within reasonable bounds, this in-group (iii)______ generally evolves into a more mature friendship pattern.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, H　peer; cliquish social behavior; clustering
cluster
cluster: standard unit headword
#213 SE
At first glance, the new CEO stands in stark contrast to his predecessor: while Simpkins came across as a scolding headmaster, his successor appears __________.At first glance, the new CEO stands in stark contrast to his predecessor: while Simpkins came across as a scolding headmaster, his successor appears __________.
答案：A, D　blithe; winsome
scrupulous
scrupulous: standard unit headword
#214 Sentence Equivalence
For wily political press officers, the art of spin is not quite supplanting truth with lies, but instead it aspires to replace awkward complexity with catchy ______; successful spin creates the impression of unavoidable common sense.For wily political press officers, the art of spin is not quite supplanting truth with lies, but instead it aspires to replace awkward complexity with catchy ______; successful spin creates the impression of unavoidable common sense.
答案：E, F　simplicity; intelligibility
supplant
supplant: standard unit headword

答案：A, B　presage; foretell

belie: standard unit headword

The limitations of human attention cause us to miss much of what goes on around us. The real problem here is that we are often (i)______ these limitations: we think that we see the world as it really is, but our ostensibly reliable visual experience (ii)______ striking mental (iii)______.The limitations of human attention cause us to miss much of what goes on around us. The real problem here is that we are often (i)______ these limitations: we think that we see the world as it really is, but our ostensibly reliable visual experience (ii)______ striking mental (iii)______.

Blank 2

答案：B, D, I　unaware of; belies; lapses

答案：B, F　adaptability; flexibility

答案：A, C　parochial; narrow

答案：A, F　asserts; maintains

答案：B, E　patent; unequivocal

Braque and Picasso created Cubism together, but the mercurial Picasso is regarded as the star of the movement while Braque, because he was so much more (ii)______ , has been relatively (iii)______ by history.The quiet, careful member of a collaborative, creative pair may naturally be (i)______ by posterity in comparison with the brash, dramatic one. 答案：A, F　ceded to; left to

答案：A, E　lacking; wanting

Blank 2

答案：A, B　minimal; trifling

答案：B, C　a cosmopolitan; an inclusive

答案：D, F　prevented; precluded

#236 Text Completion
Filler claims that after the social welfare programs of the 1960s, belief that the government has an obligation to provide decent housing for citizens who cannot afford it was (i)______ in the United States by the notion that providing suitable shelter for everyone should be (ii)______. Thus today in the (iii)______ of taxpayer-sponsored initiatives, we have volunteer home-construction programs, honorable in intent but pitifully limited in scope.Filler claims that after the social welfare programs of the 1960s, belief that the government has an obligation to provide decent housing for citizens who cannot afford it was (i)______ in the United States by the notion that providing suitable shelter for everyone should be (ii)______. Blank 2
Blank 3

答案：D, F　soft; malleable

Specifically, the pulsar’s periodic radiation pattern was initially interpreted as (iii) ______ rather than—aas it turned out to be—the natural product of the spinning of a neutron star.When pulsars were first discovered, some of the astrophysicists involved briefly (i)______ these types of stars might be (ii)______ extraterrestrial intelligence. One memory can seem (ii)______ whereas another must be coaxed out of our brain little by little. In reality, however, these claims to a radical political agenda were often (i)______, meant to give lurid thrillers the appearance of (ii)______.A hallmark of certain nineteenth-century mystery novels was the reform agenda of their authors, who ostensibly sought to expose economic injustice while depicting the seamy underside of urban life. In reality, however, these claims to a radical political agenda were often (i)______, meant to give lurid thrillers the appearance of (ii)______.

答案：B, D　expel; oust

Blank 1
Blank 2

答案：B, D　unwarranted; groundless

Blank 1
Blank 2

答案：C, E　private; insular

#268 Text Completion

There are, it seems, (iii)______ number of ways to avoid telling a straightforward story.By the end of the 1970s, the postmodern novel had degenerated from a bold attempt to (i)______ the conventions of traditional narrative into a literary style as (ii)______ as any other. There are, it seems, (iii)______ number of ways to avoid telling a straightforward story.

Blank 2
Blank 3

#269 Sentence Equivalence
While it is always clear that the author’s message is heartfelt, it is mostly buried by shortcomings of style, organization and production, although the book does become more ______ toward the end.While it is always clear that the author’s message is heartfelt, it is mostly buried by shortcomings of style, organization and production, although the book does become more ______ toward the end.
答案：B, D　intelligible; readable
voluble
voluble: standard unit headword
#270 Text Completion
Rocks of the Morrison Formation in the western United States yield many dinosaur fossils but few plant fossils. This apparent (i)______ of plants leads some scientists to speculate that the region was once mostly barren. But others have (ii)______ , noting that the herbivorous dinosaurs there would have needed an abundant food supply.Rocks of the Morrison Formation in the western United States yield many dinosaur fossils but few plant fossils. This apparent (i)______ of plants leads some scientists to speculate that the region was once mostly barren. But others have (ii)______ , noting that the herbivorous dinosaurs there would have needed an abundant food supply.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, F　dearth; demurred
apparent
apparent: standard unit headword

答案：A, D　venerated; stunned

The nature of our recollection is (i)______ . One memory can seem (ii)______ whereas another must be coaxed out of our brain little by little. Although a moment that excites our emotions is more likely to be recorded than a (iii)______ experience, the sensory qualities of an event also play a part in how vividly and accurately we remember it.The nature of our recollection is (i)______ . One memory can seem (ii)______ whereas another must be coaxed out of our brain little by little. Although a moment that excites our emotions is more likely to be recorded than a (iii)______ experience, the sensory qualities of an event also play a part in how vividly and accurately we remember it.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, E, I　inconsistent; immediate and colorful; routine

答案：A　polemical

Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, E, H　implemented; can grow unchecked; doomed

Archaeologists have shown, for example, that the largest contiguous tract of what was thought to be virgin rain forest in the southern Amazon had been transformed into a cultural parkland before European contact, and many of the forest islands in West Africa’s savanna forest transition zone are (ii)______ as well.Recent scholarship has questioned the (i)______ of tropical forests around the world. Consequently, their descendants, today's anthropoid primates, cannot make vitamin C.Researchers claim that early mammals evolved a multistep process to synthesize vitamin C in their own bodies but that tens of millions of years ago some primates became so (i)______ eating fruits rich in this vitamin that making their own became (ii)______ . Take, for example, the way channels emerge when people move in crowds. In the midst of initially (ii)______ movements, one person begins to follow another—in an effort to avoid collisions—and streams of movement emerge. As more people join in, there is greater pull on others to join the flow, and the particular channels become (iii)______ .Viewing people as “social atoms” that obey rather simple rule (which are not unlike the laws of physics), one may discover certain (i)______ . Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, F, I　lawlike regulations; chaotic; self-perpetuating

#363 Text Completion
As cheaper imports drove most California potteries out of business during the 1950s, one company (i)______. The substantial size and weight of the specialized products produced by Architectural Pottery helped (ii)______ the company, because it was uneconomical for foreign companies to ship similarly large objects to California.As cheaper imports drove most California potteries out of business during the 1950s, one company (i)______. The substantial size and weight of the specialized products produced by Architectural Pottery helped (ii)______ the company, because it was uneconomical for foreign companies to ship similarly large objects to California.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, E　flourished; insulate
stagnate
stagnate: standard unit headword
#364 Text Completion
Motivation is the hardest of all managerial tasks, and it is ______ to expect a single memo, no matter how well-crafted, to have much effect on the staff's attitude.Motivation is the hardest of all managerial tasks, and it is ______ to expect a single memo, no matter how well-crafted, to have much effect on the staff's attitude.
答案：C　fanciful
scrupulous
scrupulous: standard unit headword
#365 Text Completion
Many popular musicians have (i)______ new digital technologies that allow them unprecedented control over their music. These musicians use computers to (ii)______ and modify their songs, resulting in a level of musical precision often unattainable naturally. Of course, though, as is often the case with new technologies, some traditionalists (iii)______ these developments.Many popular musicians have (i)______ new digital technologies that allow them unprecedented control over their music. These musicians use computers to (ii)______ and modify their songs, resulting in a level of musical precision often unattainable naturally. Of course, though, as is often the case with new technologies, some traditionalists (iii)______ these developments.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, F, G　incorporated; recast; balk at
energize
energize: standard unit headword
#366 Text Completion
Since the 1920s, historical fiction writers in China have emancipated the genre from the traditional notion that (i)______ was the ultimate goal of history writing. Yet the traditional commitment to (ii)______ was not simply (iii)______ : this new genre was expected to capture the essence of historical truth even as it allowed space for the writer’s imagination.Since the 1920s, historical fiction writers in China have emancipated the genre from the traditional notion that (i)______ was the ultimate goal of history writing. Yet the traditional commitment to (ii)______ was not simply (iii)______ : this new genre was expected to capture the essence of historical truth even as it allowed space for the writer’s imagination.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, G　factuality; veracity; jettisoned

rationalize: standard unit headword
#367 Text Completion
The theory that the 1908 Tunguska event was the explosion of a cosmic body in the sky over Siberia is ______: no one has yet found fragments of the object or any impact craters in the affected region.The theory that the 1908 Tunguska event was the explosion of a cosmic body in the sky over Siberia is ______: no one has yet found fragments of the object or any impact craters in the affected region.
答案：E　unproven
plausible

#368 Text Completion
Marie Tharp’s mapping of the ocean floor, which was based on a mass of previously unanalyzed measurements of ocean depth, did not depend on (i)______ but on (ii)______ : she (iii)______ the sea floor’s contours by marrying the voluminous but incomplete data with her knowledge of geology.Marie Tharp’s mapping of the ocean floor, which was based on a mass of previously unanalyzed measurements of ocean depth, did not depend on (i)______ but on (ii)______ : she (iii)______ the sea floor’s contours by marrying the voluminous but incomplete data with her knowledge of geology.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, E, G　rote work; extrapolation; inferred

Scientists suspect that athletes may experience augmented bloodstream levels of the hormone oxytocin during competition. (i)______ considerations, however, make it difficult to (ii)______ oxytocin levels during sports events: few will willingly interrupt player in the middle of a game in order for scientists to test players’ blood.Scientists suspect that athletes may experience augmented bloodstream levels of the hormone oxytocin during competition. (i)______ considerations, however, make it difficult to (ii)______ oxytocin levels during sports events: few will willingly interrupt player in the middle of a game in order for scientists to test players’ blood.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, E　practical; quantify changes in

#370 Text Completion
The journalist was someone whose habitual distrust of authority struck a few people as (i)______ but who had enough talent and charm that most found the trait to be (ii)______, with the result that it did not become a personal or professional liability.The journalist was someone whose habitual distrust of authority struck a few people as (i)______ but who had enough talent and charm that most found the trait to be (ii)______, with the result that it did not become a personal or professional liability.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, E　pathological; pardonable

#371 Text Completion
Conventionally, the ultimate measure of a scientific work’s validity is how broadly and confidently its conclusions become accepted in the relevant field, which in turn (i)______ the extent to which its findings are replicated and extended. However, establishing such validity, especially for a novel experimental finding, can take years, and what (ii)______ replication or extension may be (iii)______ for some time.Conventionally, the ultimate measure of a scientific work’s validity is how broadly and confidently its conclusions become accepted in the relevant field, which in turn (i)______ the extent to which its findings are replicated and extended. However, establishing such validity, especially for a novel experimental finding, can take years, and what (ii)______ replication or extension may be (iii)______ for some time.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, F, H　derives from; constitutes; disputed

Blank 2
答案：C, D　elitist; undesirable

Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, D　unethical; skullduggery
